creator: indiamachinemart.in@gmail.com
text: The issue with your USB Ethernet device causing a boot hang when an NFS mount is attempted seems to stem from network connectivity problems, as indicated by the "RPC: Port mapper failure - RPC: Unable to send" errors in your console log. This suggests that the system is unable to communicate with the NFS server at the address 10.0.0.4. To resolve this issue, start by ensuring that the network connection is up and the system can reach the NFS server. You can check this by using the ping command to test connectivity to 10.0.0.4. If the network connectivity is confirmed to be functional, the next step is to verify that the NFS server itself is running correctly and is properly configured to export the desired directories. This can involve checking the NFS server's status and ensuring that the necessary services, such as rpcbind, nfs-server, and related services, are running. Additionally, confirm that the NFS exports are correctly defined and accessible. If these services are not running or if the exports are misconfigured, the client will not be able to mount the NFS file system, leading to the observed errors and subsequent boot hang. Rgards Plastic Injection moulding Machine
